personal_brand_positioning: AI automation builder helping developers and founders ship reliable workflow systems and prompt architectures.

backstory.origin: Started as a self-taught developer automating tedious workflows and documenting systems.
backstory.journey: Built SaaS tools, prompt frameworks, and automation blueprints. Pivoted focus to helping builders and teams replace manual grind with reliable AI workflows.
milestones: Over 4 years of professional development experience
Shipped multiple small automation products and toolkits
Documented agent patterns and orchestration best practices
lessons_learned: Manual grind is the most expensive hidden tax
Architecture and observability prevent silent failures
Small automations compound into big wins
Templates are useful if they’re documented and testable

personal_values: Helping builders operate like bigger teams
content_pillars: Actionable tips for developers and indie hackers
Open ended questions to spark engagement
audience_resonance: Developers, indie hackers, and founders who want reliable automations that save time and compound outcomes.
tone: Confident, slightly playful, authoritative, but still authentic and relatable.

content_preferences.storytelling_depth: Mix of punchy insights with occasional premium-level case breakdowns
topics_to_avoid: Overly academic posts without practical context

goals_with_personal_brand: Position myself as an automation developer and builder
Attract inbound users and clients for automation toolkits
Build trust through reproducible frameworks and transparent logs
Grow authority in developer and indie hacker communities
